title: Bachelor's Thesis — Polyp Detection
description: Bachelor's thesis on detecting polyps in colonoscopy images with deep learning, using CycleGAN and SPADE to generate synthetic training data when real labels were scarce. On this page: browse the training pipeline and generative augmentation samples, and interact with the live model when the backend is up — otherwise explore static results.

title: Draculin
description: Health-tracking app with an AI chatbot and computer vision assistance, built in Flutter and Django as a BitsXLaMarató 2023 finalist. On this page: walk through the features and interact with the chatbot and CV flows — running against the real backend when it's up, simulated otherwise.

title: BitsXLaMarató — Aorta
description: BitsXLaMarató 2022 winner: a tool to segment the aorta in ultrasound videos with Mask R-CNN and reconstruct it in 3D to help detect aneurysms. On this page: run the segmentation and diameter explorer — against the real backend when it's up, simulated otherwise.

title: License Plate Detection
description: Computer vision project to locate and read license plates from car photos, originally built as a classical MATLAB pipeline. On this page: run the full pipeline on sample images, ported to TypeScript and running in your browser.

title: Programming Game Viewer
description: Data structures course project: a grid-based game where C++ bots compete as warriors and builders in a class-wide tournament. On this page: watch matches replay with live C++ engine execution, Canvas rendering, and interactive playback controls.

title: JSBach Interpreter
description: Compilers course project: JSBach, a small programming language where you describe musical pieces as code, built with an ANTLR4 grammar. On this page: write JSBach code and hear it played back live through a Web Audio interpreter.

title: Tenda Online
description: Web applications course project: a full e-commerce store built in PHP and MySQL, covering product catalog, shopping cart, and checkout flow. On this page: browse the store — against the real backend when it's up, otherwise a static rebuilt version you can still explore.

title: WPGMA Clustering
description: Programming course project: implementing WPGMA clustering in C++ to build phylogenetic trees from species gene sequences. On this page: enter species and watch the algorithm merge clusters into a dendrogram — against the real C++ engine when the backend is up, or a static walkthrough otherwise.

title: MPIDS Solver
description: Algorithms project on the Minimum Positive Influence Dominating Set problem, solved with greedy heuristics and simulated-annealing-style local search. On this page: build or edit a graph in a D3 force-directed view and run the solver interactively.

title: Graph Phase Transitions
description: Algorithms research project studying connectivity and complexity phase transitions on binomial, geometric, and grid random graphs. On this page: watch real-time percolation animations and Monte Carlo sweep charts as you tune graph parameters.

title: Travel agency (PDDL)
description: Automated planning course project: a PDDL domain for booking trips with flights, hotels, numeric constraints, and metric minimization. On this page: run the planner on custom problems when the backend is up, or inspect the domain, problem files, and sample planner outputs otherwise.

title: Desastres IA
description: AI course project framing disaster relief as local search, with helicopters rescuing groups from rescue centers via hill climbing and simulated annealing. On this page: run the solver on an interactive 2D map and compare experiments in a dashboard.

title: Machine Learning — Hypothyroid
description: Machine learning course practica: training and comparing nine sklearn model families to predict hypothyroidism from clinical data. On this page: open the notebook, try a live logistic regression predictor, and classify points on a k-NN PCA projection.

title: Recommendation System
description: Software engineering course project: a content-based recommendation system built in Java with Spring Boot and Thymeleaf, including a full web interface. On this page: browse the recommendation flow and inspect the underlying Java/Spring architecture.

title: CAIM IR Explorer
description: Information retrieval course labs: computing PageRank over the global airport network and verifying Zipf's Law on text corpora. On this page: run PageRank on a D3 world map and fit Zipf curves on real text interactively.

title: Trip Planner (Expert System)
description: Knowledge-based systems course project: an expert system trip planner originally written with CLIPS production rules, reimplemented in Python. On this page: answer 10 questions about your travel preferences and get a full itinerary with cities, hotels, activities, transport, and cost.

title: Parallel Computing Labs
description: Parallel computing course labs in C with OpenMP: Mandelbrot, heat equation, 4-way merge sort, π estimation, and speedup benchmarks. On this page: run all five compiled to WebAssembly in-browser and inspect parallel scaling.

title: Robotics Dashboard
description: Robotics course labs covering odometry, wall-following, 3D arm forward kinematics (Puma560 / 3-Link), trajectory planning, sensors, and EKF state estimation. On this page: replay and interact with each lab using real captured data in a unified dashboard.

title: Algorithm Visualizer
description: Algorithms and data structures coursework covering 18 classical algorithms across graphs, sorting, search, and combinatorial problems. On this page: step through each one with interactive Canvas visualizations and adjustable inputs.

title: Computer Graphics Shader Playground
description: Computer graphics course labs originally written against desktop OpenGL, covering vertex transforms, Phong lighting, procedural textures, fragment patterns, and geometry shader emulation. On this page: edit the GLSL live in a WebGL2 playground and see the shader output update in real time.
